title: HIVE Blockchain's Ethereum Mining Increases 179 Percent
text: HIVE Blockchain Technologies released its Q1 2019 financial results, demonstrating significant growth in ethereum production. The company mined 17,555 ethereum during the quarter, representing substantial expansion from the prior period's 9,800 coins. CEO Harry Pokrandt attributed the increase to continued network capacity additions. The Sweden facility added 6.8 megawatts of capacity, bringing total capacity to 24.2 MW. Pokrandt stated: The scale of this property is tremendous. This site gives us a large opportunity to develop at both a scale and a pace that we want. The company generated US$10.6 million in revenue with a 60 percent gross mining margin. Pokrandt announced a 200 petahash cloud-based ASIC agreement offering flexibility in deployment location, noting North America is on the company's radar. The announcement also included management changes: Pokrandt retired as president and CEO effective August 31, with Frank Holmes assuming interim executive chairman duties and Olivier Roussy Newton becoming interim president. Following the results, HIVE shares declined 10.47 percent, closing at C$0.77.
